english: = ₒtsór-ba (tshor ba) to perceive, to scent, taste, hear, see, e.g. *daṅ-mo (grang mo) rag* (rag) I feel cold, *daṅ-mo (grang mo) rag-ga* (rag ga) do you feel cold? (but *daṅ-mo (grang mo) dug* (dug) it is cold); *gó-la (mgo la) zug (zug) rag* (rag) (C. *rig* (rig)) my head aches; *tóg-ri (ltogs ri) rag* (rag) I feel hungry, *tóg-ri (ltogs ri) rág-ga* (rag ga) are you hungry? *ṅai (nga'i) miṅ (ming) żöd-da ('bod da) rag* (rag) I hear my name called; *go (sgo) kád-da ('khad da) rag* (rag) I perceive the door sticks; *i (i) luṅ-po (rlung po) kyér-ra (khyer ra) rag* (rag) I see, the wind will carry that away; *go (sgo) ṗé-te (phye te) mi (mi) rag* (rag) the door seems to be locked. — 2. adj. dark-russet, brownish, of rocks, horses W.

1. W. adj. to the gerund *rág-te* (rag te) (*sbrág-ste* (sbrag ste)): *be-rág (be rag) yu-dán* (g.yu ldan) (lit. ydan) *rág-te* (rag te) a fillet together with a strip set with turkoises. — 2. prop. n. of a village Mil.
a bead of a rosary, acc. to Liš. from Elaeocarpus Janitrus, the berries of which are used for such beads.

1. dam, mole, dike, embankment, also ču-rágs (chu rags), ču-lón (chu lon) — 2. any construction of a similar shape: pág-rags (phag rags) (also pág-ra (phag ra)) intrenchment, breast-work; pùb-rags (phub rags) stack, rick; šiṅ-rágs (shing rags) stack of wood.

coarse, thick, gross, lús-kyi (lus kyi) rnám-pa (rnam pa) pṙa-rágs-rnams (phra rags rnams) Wdṅ. the more delicate and the coarser component parts of the body; rags-pai (rags pa'i) dbáṅ-du (dbang du) byás-na (byas na) Wdṅ., reckoning one with another, on an average; rough, as in: rágs-rtsis-su (rags rtsis su) by a rough estimate Tar.; rágs-pai (rags pa'i) mi-rtág-pa (mi rtag pa) daṅ (dang) pṙá-bai (phra ba'i) mi-rtág-pa (mi rtag pa) the perishableness of the whole mass and of the single parts Thgy.; yán-lag (yan lag) rágs-pa (rags pa) prob.: strong, firm limbs Pth.; of Buddhas is said that they appear rágs-pai (rags pa'i) tsúl-gyis (tshul gyis) i.e. bodily, or substantially; rags-ris (rags ris) byed-pa (byed pa) Sch.: to work, mould, form, sketch etc. roughly.

1. self B. and col. (ñyid (nyid), with few exceptions, is, in W. at least, colloquially not in use) ṅa-ráṅ (nga rang) k̇yod-ráṅ (khyod rang) I myself, thou thyself etc., in col. language also = I, thou etc.; sometimes the person is only indicated by the context, the pronoun I etc. being omitted; raṅ-čag (rang cag), ráṅ-rnams (rang rnams) plur.; ráṅ-gi (rang gi) my, thy etc.; čúṅ-ma (chung ma) de (de) ráṅ-gi (rang gi) lús-la (lus la) čágs-pas (chags pas) this wife fond of herself, in love with herself Dzl. (yet cf. de-ráṅ (de rang), below); des (des) ráṅ-gi (rang gi) ma (ma) yín-par (yin par) rig-nas (rig nas) he perceiving that it was his own mother Pth.; ráṅ-la (rang la) ráṅ-gis (rang gis) skra (skra) bčád-de (bcad de) shaving one's own head Dzl.; also in a gen. sense: ráṅ-bas (rang bas) ṅán-pa (ngan pa) an inferior person than one's self Thgy., in like manner: ráṅ-las (rang las) čé-ba (che ba) Thgr.; ráṅ-la (rang la) bu (bu) méd-na (med na) if a man has no son of his own Mil.; ráṅ-gi (rang gi) srúṅ-ba (srung ba) to keep, to guard one's own property Thgy.; *raṅ (rang) mi-dód-pe (mi 'dod pa'i) kyen (rkyen) tsáṅ-ma* (tshang ma) C. all the disagreeable things that fall to one's lot; in compounds: raṅ-séms (rang sems) one's own soul (opp. to yżan-lús (gzhan lus)) Mil.; v. also dré-ba ('dre ba) extr.; raṅ-rig (rang rig) raṅ-gsal (rang gsal) raṅ-bde (rang bde) gsum (gsum) self-created knowledge, clearness, and happiness (the three fruits of the spirit) Mil.; raṅ-sróg (rang srog) ráṅ-gis (rang gis) gčod (gcod) you will take your own life Glr. — 2. spontaneously, of one's own accord, żal-zás (zhal zas) raṅ-óṅ-ṅo (rang 'ong ngo) Dzl.; ráṅ-byon-pa (rang byon pa), ráṅ-byuṅ-ba (rang byung ba) originated of itself, v. below; raṅ gról-ba (rang grol ba) 1. to get loose, come loose of itself. 2. to become clear or intelligible spontaneously, by intuition. 3. to save one's self; ráṅ-šar-ba (rang shar ba) = ráṅ-grol-ba (rang grol ba) 2. — 3. just, exactly, precisely, the very, de ráṅ (de rang) the very same; de raṅ yín (de rang yin) so it is! exactly so! just so! *dhá-ta (da lta) raṅ* (rang) C., *dá-či (da ci) raṅ* (rang), *dág-sa (dag sa) raṅ* (rang) W. just-now, *di-riṅ (de ring) raṅ* (rang) just to-day W.; already, sṅá-mo (snga mo) raṅ (rang) already early in the morning Mil.; barely, merely, the mere, the very, ṅa (nga) daṅ (dang) pṙád-pa (phrad pa) ráṅ-gis (rang gis) by the mere meeting with me Mil.; mi (mi) raṅ (rang) a person travelling all alone, i.e. without baggage, horse or companion Kun.; mo-ráṅ (mo rang) v. mo. — really, indeed, actually, truly (the verb being repeated): mi-la-rás-pa (mi la ras pa) de (de) yín (yin) raṅ (rang) yín-nam? (yin nam) art thou really that same Milaraspa? *yoṅ (yong) raṅ (rang) yoṅ-gyu (yong rgyu) yín* (yin) C. he will truly or certainly come; even, sdáṅ-po (sdang po) raṅ (rang) byas (byas) now they even hated him Mil. Comp. raṅ-skal (rang skal) a person's own share. — raṅ skyu (rang skyu) (?) túb-pa (thub pa) Sch.: to act after one's own mind. — raṅ-skyur (rang skyur) vinegar Cs.(?) <
